game_info.title: Sugar, Sugar - Physics Puzzle Game
game_info.concept: sugar, sugar is a physics-based puzzle game where players draw lines on the screen with a single finger to guide a continuous stream of white sugar particles into designated cups. Sugar trickles from a source at the top, affected by realistic gravity, and drawn lines act as physical barriers to redirect its flow. Each of the 125 levels requires a specific amount of sugar to be collected in all cups, displayed on the UI. Progression introduces new mechanics like color filters (requiring sugar to change color before entering a matching cup), gravity switches, and teleports, increasing puzzle complexity. A level is won by filling all cups to their required capacity, and lost if the sugar source depletes before all cups are filled.
game_info.description: Guide falling sugar particles into cups by drawing lines to redirect their flow. Each level requires filling all cups to their target amounts. Sugar flows continuously from the top, and your drawn lines act as physical barriers. Watch out for color filters that change sugar colors, and match the colored sugar to the correct cups. Plan your lines carefully - once the sugar runs out, it's game over!
game_info.controls:
Mouse: Draw lines to guide sugar (disabled in keyboard-only mode)
Arrow Keys: Navigate and adjust line drawing cursor
Space: Place/remove line segment at cursor position
Shift: Clear all drawn lines
R: Restart level (from game over screen)
Enter: Start game (from start screen)
Esc: Pause/Unpause game

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic sugar particle physics and line drawing mechanics. Verifies that sugar falls from the source, respects gravity, and collides with drawn lines properly. Tests if the player can successfully draw lines that redirect sugar flow.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Automatically draws a simple diagonal line from left to right below the sugar source. Lets sugar particles fall and interact with the line for several seconds. Monitors particle behavior and collision responses.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Sugar particles should fall from the source, collide with the drawn line, and be redirected. Particles should bounce and roll realistically along the line. No particles should pass through the line. Test succeeds if particles consistently interact with drawn barriers.

TEST_1.expected: Sugar particles should fall from the source, collide with the drawn line, and be redirected. Particles should bounce and roll realistically along the line. No particles should pass through the line. Test succeeds if particles consistently interact with drawn barriers.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by strategically filling cups to completion. Verifies that the game correctly detects when all cups reach their target fill amounts and transitions to the win state. Tests the core victory mechanic.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Draws optimal lines that funnel all sugar directly into the cups efficiently. Creates a path system that maximizes sugar collection. Monitors cup fill levels until all targets are met.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: All cups should fill to their required amounts. Game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state when targets are reached. Win screen should display with congratulations message and level completion stats. Test succeeds when win condition triggers correctly.
TEST_2.description: Testing win condition by strategically filling cups to completion. Verifies that the game correctly detects when all cups reach their target fill amounts and transitions to the win state. Tests the core victory mechanic.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing lose condition when sugar depletes before cups are filled. Verifies that the game tracks remaining sugar particles and triggers game over when the source runs dry with unfilled cups.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Draws lines that deliberately redirect sugar away from cups or create inefficient paths. Allows sugar to fall off screen or collect in wrong areas. Waits for sugar source to fully deplete.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Sugar source should eventually stop producing particles. Game should detect incomplete cup filling and transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state. Lose screen should display with remaining targets shown. Test succeeds when lose condition triggers appropriately.
TEST_3.description: Testing lose condition when sugar depletes before cups are filled. Verifies that the game tracks remaining sugar particles and triggers game over when the source runs dry with unfilled cups.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing color filter mechanics that transform sugar particles. Verifies that particles passing through color filters change to the appropriate color and that colored cups only accept matching colored sugar.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Draws lines guiding sugar through color filters before directing it to colored cups. Tests both correct color matching (should fill cup) and incorrect matching (should not count). Monitors particle color changes and cup acceptance logic.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Particles should visually change color when passing through filters. Colored cups should only increment fill amount for matching colored particles. Mismatched particles should not contribute to cup filling. Test succeeds when color matching works correctly.
TEST_4.description: Testing color filter mechanics that transform sugar particles. Verifies that particles passing through color filters change to the appropriate color and that colored cups only accept matching colored sugar.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing line drawing limits and constraints. Verifies that players cannot draw unlimited lines and that the drawing budget system works correctly. Tests line removal and redrawing capabilities.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Repeatedly attempts to draw lines until the budget is exhausted. Tests the clear all lines function (Shift key). Verifies that removed lines free up budget for new drawings.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Drawing should stop when budget reaches zero. UI should accurately display remaining line capacity. Clearing lines should restore budget. Test succeeds when drawing constraints are properly enforced and feedback is clear.
TEST_5.description: Testing line drawing limits and constraints. Verifies that players cannot draw unlimited lines and that the drawing budget system works correctly. Tests line removal and redrawing capabilities.
TEST_5.strategy: Repeatedly attempts to draw lines until the budget is exhausted. Tests the clear all lines function (Shift key). Verifies that removed lines free up budget for new drawings.
TEST_5.expected: Drawing should stop when budget reaches zero. UI should accurately display remaining line capacity. Clearing lines should restore budget. Test succeeds when drawing constraints are properly enforced and feedback is clear.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing pause functionality and game state management. Verifies that pausing stops all physics simulation, particle generation, and game logic while maintaining the current state for resumption.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Starts gameplay, draws some lines, waits for sugar to flow, then triggers pause. Monitors that all movement ceases. Unpauses and verifies gameplay resumes correctly from the paused state.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: When paused, all particles should freeze in place, no new particles should spawn, and physics should halt. Upon unpause, everything should resume smoothly. UI should clearly indicate paused state. Test succeeds when pause/unpause works without glitches.
TEST_6.description: Testing pause functionality and game state management. Verifies that pausing stops all physics simulation, particle generation, and game logic while maintaining the current state for resumption.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing multiple cups with different target amounts. Verifies that the game correctly tracks individual cup progress and requires all cups to be filled before winning, not just reaching a total sugar amount.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Creates level with multiple cups at different positions with varying target amounts. Draws lines that fill cups unevenly. Monitors individual cup progress tracking and win condition evaluation.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Each cup should independently track its fill percentage. Game should not trigger win until ALL cups meet their individual targets. UI should clearly show progress for each cup. Test succeeds when multi-cup logic works correctly.
TEST_7.description: Testing multiple cups with different target amounts. Verifies that the game correctly tracks individual cup progress and requires all cups to be filled before winning, not just reaching a total sugar amount.
